title: Pagination, Filtering, Links, Rate Limits, Versioning, and Resumable Work · Go Client Systems · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A platform SDK team integrating a staging control plane must build a first protocol model from narrated fixtures and explain each policy boundary for pagination, filtering, links, rate limits, versioning, and resumable work, producing resumable rate-aware API traversal. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a Go 1.26 net/http resumable rate-aware API traversal while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practic…
objectives: Traverse offset, page-number, cursor, and link pagination while detecting repeats, gaps, and termination. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Encode repeated parameters, arrays, dates, sorting, and filters according to the API contract without manual concatenation. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Coordinate server guidance, local quotas, concurrency, backoff, and caller priorities without synchronized bursts. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pagination Models
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Go staging control-plane client follows cursor c3, repeated link c3, and rate limit 30/minute while records change between pages. Encode filters, detect loops, checkpoint stable identity, resume after failure, and avoid duplicates or gaps. Competency probe: Traverse offset, page-number, cursor, and link pagination while detecting repeats, gaps, and termination. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pagination Models.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for traverse offset, page-number, cursor, and link pagination while detecting repeats, gaps, and termination. demonstrate the result through go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, roundtripper, and httptest evidence. For Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pagination Models, keep Pagination Models connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: Go staging control-plane client follows cursor c3, repeated link c3, and rate limit 30/minute while records change between pages.
